hypotheses.title: Geochemical Peroxidation Potential Index — mineral-catalyzed rate constants predict ferroptosis sensitivity
hypotheses.key_claim: Ferrihydrite-surface Fenton k values predict RSL3 EC50 across cell lines (r > 0.7)

hypotheses.title: Ferrihydrite nanoparticles recapitulate ferroptosis PLOOH profile in synthetic protocells
hypotheses.key_claim: PLOOH positional isomers from ferrihydrite-vesicle system match ferroptosis signature

hypotheses.title: GPX4 selenocysteine evolved to outcompete serpentinization-pH Fenton kinetics
hypotheses.key_claim: GPX4 Sec/Cys activity ratio increases >5x from pH 7.4 to pH 10 (serpentinization conditions)

hypotheses.title: PHREEQC geochemical speciation code predicts labile iron pool dynamics in ferroptosis
hypotheses.key_claim: PHREEQC iron speciation model outperforms one-compartment LIP models for ferroptosis Fe(II)/Fe(III) dynamics

hypotheses.title: Fe(II)/Fe(III) ≈ 1 is a universal peroxidation tipping point conserved from geology to biology
hypotheses.key_claim: Ferroptosis onset and mineral-system maximum peroxidation both occur at Fe(II)/Fe(III) ≈ 0.8-1.2

diversity_check: PASS — 5 distinct angles: quantitative prediction, experimental chemistry, evolutionary biology, tool transfer, universal physics
